explanation: All Section 2 criteria are satisfied. 2.1: Confirmed cervical radiculopathy at C5-C6 with nerve root compression and dermatomal radicular symptoms, supported by MRI within 6 months. 2.2(a): 8 weeks of supervised PT directed at the cervical condition with documented functional outcomes (exceeds 6-week minimum). 2.2(b): Two qualifying medications trialed for at least 4 weeks each (naproxen NSAID for 6 weeks, gabapentin neuropathic agent for 5 weeks). 2.2(c): One cervical epidural steroid injection at C5-C6. 2.3: MRI within 6 months interpreted by a radiologist showing pathology consistent with symptoms and surgical level (interpretation by radiologist OR treating specialist suffices). 2.4: Physician provided attestation, clinical rationale, functional limitations, and statement of surgical risks and alternatives. Therefore authorization is approved.
